steps.title: Identify the recurring pattern
steps.content: The sentence after the blank says that "as decades passed, new calculations would reveal increasingly precise coordinates, yet the prime meridian remained unchanged." The habitual phrase "would reveal" signals a repeated cycle: new data appeared, the official meridian stayed put, and that loop ran for decades until the 1980s finally broke it.

steps.title: Pick the transition that captures repetition
steps.content: The needed transition marks that recurring, again-and-again quality of new calculations being produced without prompting change.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Specifically,': signals a single concrete example of the prior idea, but the blank introduces a long-running pattern, not a specification. ✗
  • Choice B — 'To that end,': signals purposeful action toward a goal, but no goal from the prior sentence is being pursued. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Again and again,': directly names the recurring nature of the new calculations across decades. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Granted,': concedes a counterpoint, but the blank introduces continuation, not concession. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
